title: calluna vulgaris f. foxii
intro: Calluna vulgaris f. foxii, also known as fox heather, is a compact and low-growing shrub belonging to the heather family (Ericaceae). The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can aid in wound healing and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of calluna vulgaris f. foxii include its potential to reduce fever, ease digestive issues, and act as an expectorant to help clear mucus from the lungs. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to support immune function and alleviate cold and flu symptoms.
